name: linCsError Selectors
title: linCsError Selectors
return: Time stamp synchronized with the global time base on the
tables: Time stamp synchronized with the global time base on the computer (CAN hardware or PC system clock). Unit: 10µs
Time stamp synchronized with the global time base on the computer (CAN hardware or PC system clock). Unit: nanoseconds

Time stamp generated by the LIN hardware. This time stamp is unsynchronized with the global time base on the computer and thus still original. It can be used to compare the time of two LIN hardware generated events. Unit: 10µs

Status of MsgOrigTime: Bit 0 set Time stamp is valid Bit 0 not set Time stamp is invalid Bit 1 set Time stamp was generated by software Bit 1 not set Time stamp was generated by hardware Bit 4 Has a bus-specific meaning; not currently in use for LIN
Time stamp was generated by software
Time stamp was generated by hardware
Has a bus-specific meaning; not currently in use for LIN
Time stamp was generated by software
Time stamp was generated by hardware
Has a bus-specific meaning; not currently in use for LIN
Frame header transmission time. Unit: bit time
Entire frame transmission time. Unit: bit time
LIN Frame identifier (6 bits) Value range: 0..63
Number of data bytes contained in the frame (Data Length Code). Value range: 0..8
Channel through which the checksum error has been received. Value range: 1..32
Direction of transmission: Tx: Frame was transmitted by LIN hardwareRX: Frame was receivedTXRQ: Transmission request
This flag specifies whether the frame was simulated. Values:0: Not simulated1: Simulated
Identifier of the FSM that transmitted the frame response. Only valid if lin_FSMState <> 255. Value range: 0..63
The status, in which the FSM transmitted the frame response. Value range: 0..255 If lin_FsmState = 255 no FSM was configured to respond to this frame. The value of lin_FSMId is thus also invalid.
Frame data byte (8 bit). Possible values for x: 0..7
Frame data word (16 bit). Possible values for x: 0-6. The index is byte-oriented. For example, word(1) references the data consisting of bytes 1-2 and not of bytes 2-3.
Frame data double word (32 bit). Possible values for x: 0-4. The index is byte-oriented. For example, dword(1) references the data consisting of bytes 1-4 and not of bytes 4-7.
Length of the sync break in ns
Length of the sync delimiter in ns
End of header time stamp in ns
End of byte time stamp of databyte <i> in ns Possible indexes: 0..7
Start of Frame time stamp in ns
The measured baudrate of the LIN header of this event.Unit: Bit/s
